title: Manual K-fold GBDT control model on step-65 and stop-debt features
summary: Build a minimal reproducible LightGBM or GBDT K-fold pipeline as a control against the fixed AutoGluon holdout wrapper.
rationale: All successful scored nodes are AutoGluon, while 34 non-AutoGluon/manual nodes failed or produced no score. The AutoGluon configuration was effectively constant: medium-quality AG, XGB/GBM/CAT, time_limit=600, auto_stack=False, fit_weighted_ensemble=False, and holdout validation. This means modeling diversity has not been properly tested.
implementation_hint: Implement a minimal manual LightGBM 5-fold GroupKFold pipeline using the compact step-65 feature set, optionally plus strict stop-debt features. Use binary objective and AUC metric. Report OOF ROC AUC, train fold models, rank-average test predictions, and keep all preprocessing target-free. Add CatBoost or XGBoost only after the LightGBM control is reproducible.
expected_effect: Medium potential improvement in ROC AUC and validation stability through OOF predictions, fold averaging, early stopping, and stronger control over training. Confidence is medium-low because previous manual attempts were unstable.
risk: Manual pipelines can easily introduce leakage, especially through encodings and fold-specific preprocessing. The experiment may require longer debugging. If OOF AUC is worse by more than about 0.0002 compared with the step-65 holdout, or if the pipeline is not reproducible, reject this direction.
